summary: DIA proteomics pipeline correctly configured. FDR controls applied at PSM, peptide, and protein levels. Spectral library provenance documented and validated. No significant concerns identified.

findings.title: Spectronaut calibration strategy could be improved with iRT peptides
findings.message: Retention time calibration uses a local regression model from the spectral library. Adding spiked-in iRT peptides (Biognosys iRT kit) would improve RT calibration accuracy, especially for samples with complex matrices or aggressive gradients. Current approach is acceptable but not optimal.
findings.impact: Minor RT calibration improvement possible. No correctness issue.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding iRT spike-in for better calibration. Not required for current pipeline quality.
findings.code_snippet:
rt_calibration:
  method: local_regression
  # Optional: add irt_peptides: /refs/irt_library.xlsx

findings.title: Consider enabling cross-run normalisation for inter-batch comparison
findings.message: Cross-run normalisation is disabled. If samples span multiple LC-MS batches, systematic intensity offsets between batches may bias fold change estimates. For single-batch experiments this is not a concern.
findings.impact: Potential batch effect if samples span multiple acquisition days. No issue for single-batch experiments.
findings.recommendation: If experiment spans multiple days, enable cross-run normalisation or apply batch correction (ComBat-seq) post-acquisition.
findings.code_snippet:
normalisation:
  cross_run: false  # consider enabling for multi-batch
